 {"id":"br-xj5oi","title":"ATC rollup refresh drops compacted history for touched stratum","description":"Attack surface: ATC learning loop + retention/rollup interaction.

Invariant broken:
- docs and code assume rollups outlive compacted raw rows.
- after retention_compact() deletes old terminal rows, a later refresh_rollups() on the same stratum recomputes from surviving raw rows only and overwrites the preserved rollup entry. historical totals silently shrink.

Concrete repro:
1. Insert one resolved liveness/probe experience old enough to compact.
2. refresh_rollups() -> rollup total_count=1,resolved_count=1.
3. retention_compact(..., preserve_rollups=true) -> raw row deleted, rollup still 1/1.
4. Insert a new resolved liveness/probe experience.
5. refresh_rollups() again.
6. Expected: total_count=2,resolved_count=2.
7. Actual: rollup is recomputed from surviving raw rows and drops historical compacted totals.

Local repro harness added in worktree during review: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/atc_queries.rs test name refresh_rollups_can_erase_compacted_history_for_touched_stratum (ignored because bug is known and currently failing).

Impact:
- ATC policy engine can undercount historical outcomes after compaction.
- replay/retention/rollup views diverge, making the learning loop lie about sample size and outcome mix.
- any downstream explain/simulate/policy decisions that trust rollups can be biased low after compaction.

Likely fix direction:
- introduce durable per-stratum compaction watermarks / cumulative baselines, or
- make rollups append-only with delta application instead of absolute recompute from remaining raw rows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"IvorySummit","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:10:43.757824273Z","created_by":"NavySummit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:15:55.967352153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:15:55.966849842Z","close_reason":"Already completed by 6e291c1c and df9b1492; current atc_queries.rs contains compacted rollup baselines plus regression coverage for the repro.","closed_by_session":"IvorySummit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","retention","review-r3"]}
 {"id":"br-xqs2q","title":"T1: Search cluster tool description parity","description":"Copy the EXACT Python docstring for each tool in the Search cluster into the Rust #[tool(description = "...")] attribute.

 {"id":"br-xuvzq","title":"T13.1: Implement mouse drag-and-drop for messages with drop zone highlighting","description":"Implement mouse-based drag-and-drop for the most common use case: dragging a message
to re-thread it (move to a different thread).

MOUSE DND FLOW:
1. Click and hold on a message in Messages/Threads screen for 200ms -> start drag
2. Ghost of message follows cursor (message subject text, semi-transparent)
3. Hover over thread list -> thread items highlight as valid drop zones
4. Release on a thread -> update message's thread_id via internal tool dispatch
5. Release elsewhere -> cancel, message returns to original position

DROP ZONE RENDERING:
- Valid drop zones get palette.selection_bg background
- Invalid zones get palette.severity_warn border flash
- Currently hovered valid zone gets palette.panel_border_focused border

ERROR HANDLING:
- If tool dispatch fails (e.g., permission error), show error toast
- If dropped on same thread (no change), do nothing silently
- If source message not found, show warning toast

FILES: tui_app.rs (drag state), tui_screens/messages.rs, tui_screens/threads.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Click-and-hold initiates drag after 200ms
- [ ] Ghost message follows cursor
- [ ] Valid drop zones highlight
- [ ] Drop on thread re-threads message
- [ ] Cancel on invalid drop zone

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BluePuma","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:59:54.596028994Z","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:16:21.931896373Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T17:16:21.931877859Z","close_reason":"Implemented drag-and-drop rethreading flow with unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["drag-drop","interaction","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-xuvzq","depends_on_id":"br-2bzbl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1184,"issue_id":"br-xuvzq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented mouse drag-and-drop rethread flow across messages/threads screens and app dispatch: 200ms hold-to-drag state machine, drag ghost rendering, drop-zone highlighting, drop dispatch to rethread_message op, DB thread_id update path, and no-op/invalid drop cancellation semantics. Added unit tests in tui_bridge.rs, tui_app.rs, tui_screens/messages.rs, and tui_screens/threads.rs for hold promotion, valid/invalid drop behavior, and op-arg parsing. Validation: rch remote offload is unstable for this repo due sibling path-dependency sync constraints; attempted rch exec multiple times. One rch run failed-open to local and passed targeted tests (parse_rethread_operation_arg). Additional local package checks passed: cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --all-targets and targeted drag/rethread tests. Clippy -D warnings fails on pre-existing core lints outside this bead scope.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:16:12Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-xvor4","title":"Self-update E2E should exercise >16MiB streaming tarball","description":"Recent self-update code switched tar.xz release downloads to request_streaming so legitimate archives larger than the old asupersync 16 MiB non-streaming cap work.  {"id":"br-xy39","title":"HTTP CORS parity (dev defaults + allowlist)","description":"## Objective
Implement CORS behavior parity for HTTP server.

## Scope
- Env vars: `HTTP_CORS_ENABLED`, `HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS`, `HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS`, `HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS`, `HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS`.
- Default: enabled in development, disabled in production (legacy behavior).
- Allowlist handling: empty origins → `*`.

## Tests
- Integration tests for preflight OPTIONS and response headers.
- E2E coverage in HTTP suite.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Capture CORS headers under `tests/artifacts/http/cors/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. CORS headers match legacy defaults and configuration.
2. Preflight responses are correct (status and headers).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:19:01.692103864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:20:28.309033007Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:20:28.309016766Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of br-1bm.7 (CORS parity already tracked)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-xy39","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-y3sk","title":"T8.1: Define native verify-live contract (checks, severities, JSON schema, exit codes)","description":"## Objective
Define the canonical contract for native live deployment verification so behavior is explicit before implementation.

## Work
- Specify check categories: local bundle integrity, remote endpoint availability, header/security posture, optional asset probes.
- Define pass/warn/fail severity mapping and exit code rules.
- Define JSON report schema and human-readable summary format.
- Define timeout/retry defaults and override semantics.

## Deliverable
Versioned verification contract that implementation and tests must satisfy.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, error taxonomy, and exit-code behavior for downstream implementation.
- Captures parity mapping to legacy behavior, including intentional deviations and technical rationale.
- Includes a concrete validation strategy (unit + integration + e2e) and detailed logging/artifact expectations for downstream tasks.
- Is self-contained and executable without referring back to external markdown plan documents.
- Leaves no ambiguous requirements that would permit incompatible implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:40.441739931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:11:24.631933572Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:11:15.647187579Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deploy","share","spec"],"comments":[{"id":1185,"issue_id":"br-y3sk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Delivered: docs/SPEC-verify-live-contract.md + Rust types + 15 unit tests. Contract defines 3 stages (local/remote/security), 14 checks, 4 severity levels (error/warning/info/skipped), JSON schema v1.0.0, exit code policy, timeout/retry semantics, CLI interface. Added VerifyLiveReport/VerifyLiveCheck/VerifyStage/VerifyVerdict/VerifyConfig/VerifySummary types to deploy.rs with compute_verdict(), compute_summary(), exit_code() methods. CheckSeverity extended with Skipped variant. 198 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:11:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-y999","title":"T4.4: Wire am check-inbox CLI subcommand with --agent/--rate-limit/--direct/--json flags","description":"## Objective
Expose inbox-check capabilities through a cohesive CLI surface for operator and hook workflows.

## Work
- Wire `--agent`, `--rate-limit`, `--direct`, and `--json` options into unified command behavior.
- Ensure mode selection (remote/direct) is explicit, predictable, and well-diagnosed.
- Preserve stable exit semantics and machine-readable output contracts.

## Deliverable
A complete `am check-inbox` command that replaces legacy hook script usage.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:57.283584630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:50:18.382526509Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:50:18.382492776Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-y999","depends_on_id":"br-1rzt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-y999","depends_on_id":"br-1su8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-y999","depends_on_id":"br-364e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1186,"issue_id":"br-y999","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T4.4: Wire am check-inbox CLI Subcommand

## What to build
Add a `CheckInbox` variant to the CLI enum that combines rate limiting with either
HTTP or direct DB inbox checking.

## CLI interface
```
am check-inbox [--agent NAME] [--rate-limit 30s] [--direct] [--json]
               [--host H] [--port P]
```

Flags:
- --agent: Agent name to check inbox for (default: AGENT_NAME env var)
- --rate-limit: Minimum interval between checks (default: 30s, 0 to disable)
- --direct: Query DB directly instead of HTTP (for co-located setups)
- --json: Machine-readable JSON output
- --host/--port: Server address for HTTP mode (default: 127.0.0.1:8765)

## Execution flow
1. Parse agent name (flag or env var, error if neither)
2. Check rate limiter → exit 0 silently if too soon
3. If --direct: call check_inbox_direct()
   Else: call check_inbox_http()
4. Format and print result

## Human-readable output
```
Inbox for BlueLake: 3 unread
  Latest: "Build failed on main" from RedFox (2m ago)
```

If no unread: print nothing (silent success for hook usage).

## Exit codes
- 0: Check completed (regardless of unread count)
- 0: Rate limited (skipped, nothing to do)
- 1: Error (connection failed, agent not found, etc.)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (Cli enum addition)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/check_inbox.rs (run_check_inbox_command)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:32:05Z"},{"id":1187,"issue_id":"br-y999","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION from deep audit

The CLI wiring for check-inbox needs these additional features from the bash:

1. URGENCY DETECTION:
   After fetching inbox, count messages with importance:"urgent" or importance:"high".
   If urgent_count > 0, prepend output with "⚠️ {urgent_count} urgent" warning.

2. EMOJI OUTPUT:
   - "📬 {count} unread messages" when there are unread messages
   - "⚠️ {urgent_count} urgent" when urgent messages exist
   - Silent (no output at all) when inbox is empty

3. TEMPLATE DETECTION:
   Before making any request, check AGENT_MAIL_PROJECT and AGENT_MAIL_AGENT for patterns:
   - Starts with "YOUR_"
   - Starts with "PLACEHOLDER"
   - Contains "<" and ">"
   If any match, exit silently (not an error, just unconfigured).

4. FAILURE SILENCE:
   ALL errors (connection refused, auth failure, timeout, parse error) → exit 0 silently.
   The hook must NEVER interrupt agent work with error output.
   Only produce output when there IS unread mail.

5. DIRECT DB QUERY MODE (T4.3):
   When --direct flag is used (or auto-detected when running on same machine),
   bypass HTTP and query SQLite directly for unread count.
   This is the fast path for local development.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:51:00Z"},{"id":1188,"issue_id":"br-y999","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: Track 4 Multiple Fixes

### T4.1 Rate Limiter — Lockfile Path Fix
The lockfile path is NOT project-hash-based. The ACTUAL format:
  /tmp/mcp-mail-check-{AGENT_SANITIZED}
Where AGENT_SANITIZED = agent name with non-alphanumeric chars replaced by underscore.
Example: AGENT="BlueLake" → /tmp/mcp-mail-check-BlueLake
Example: AGENT="my-agent.v2" → /tmp/mcp-mail-check-my_agent_v2

The file STORES a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) as text content.
NOT using mtime — reads the file contents with `cat`.

Logic:
1. If file exists, read contents → LAST_CHECK
2. ELAPSED = NOW - LAST_CHECK
3. If ELAPSED < INTERVAL → exit 0 silently
4. Otherwise, write NOW to file and proceed

### T4.2 JSON-RPC Client — Additional Fixes
1. JSON-RPC id is STRING "1", not integer 1
2. curl has --max-time 3 (3-second timeout) — Rust should match this
3. URL default is http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/ (note: /api/ not /mcp/)
4. fetch_inbox arguments include limit:10, include_bodies:false
5. project_key and agent_name need JSON escaping (Rust: serde_json handles this)
6. On curl failure: returns empty string, then exits 0 silently

### T4.4 CLI Wiring — Output Format Precision
The EXACT output format (every character matters for hook compatibility):

When unread mail exists WITH urgency:
```
<blank line>
📬 === INBOX REMINDER ===
⚠️  You have {N} message(s) in your inbox ({M} urgent/high priority)
   Use fetch_inbox to check your messages!
=========================
<blank line>
```

When unread mail exists WITHOUT urgency:
```
<blank line>
📬 === INBOX REMINDER ===
   You have {N} recent message(s) in your inbox.
   Consider checking with fetch_inbox if you haven't lately.
=========================
<blank line>
```

When no mail: COMPLETE SILENCE (no output at all, exit 0)

MSG_COUNT detection: counts messages in the JSON-RPC response.
The bash uses python3 or grep to extract this. Rust should parse
the JSON-RPC response with serde_json.

Urgency detection: grep for "importance":"urgent" or "importance":"high"
in the raw response string. Rust should parse the messages array and
check the importance field properly.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:03:41Z"}]}

@@ -94,8 +94,10 @@ ASSET_DIR="${MOCK_ROOT}/releases/download/v${CURRENT_VERSION}"
 ASSET_PATH="${ASSET_DIR}/${ASSET_NAME}"
 mkdir -p "${ASSET_DIR}"
 
-# Keep payload intentionally tiny to avoid HTTP body-size limits while still
-# exercising checksum/replace behavior in the self-update path.
+# Include deterministic incompressible padding so the mocked tarball exceeds
+# the old asupersync non-streaming 16 MiB body limit. That keeps this E2E tied
+# to the streaming self-update path instead of only proving tiny artifact
+# replacement.
 PAYLOAD_STAGE="${WORK}/payload"
 mkdir -p "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}"
 cat > "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}/am" <<EOF
@@ -144,7 +146,39 @@ echo "mcp-agent-mail ${CURRENT_VERSION}"
 EOF
 chmod +x "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}/mcp-agent-mail"
 
-tar -cJf "${ASSET_PATH}" -C "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}" am mcp-agent-mail
+STREAMING_SENTINEL_SIZE=$((17 * 1024 * 1024))
+python3 - "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}/streaming-sentinel.bin" "${STREAMING_SENTINEL_SIZE}" <<'PY'
+import hashlib
+import sys
+
+path = sys.argv[1]
+remaining = int(sys.argv[2])
+counter = 0
+
+with open(path, "wb") as fh:
+    while remaining > 0:
+        block = hashlib.sha256(counter.to_bytes(8, "little")).digest()
+        size = min(len(block), remaining)
+        fh.write(block[:size])
+        remaining -= size
+        counter += 1
+PY
+
+tar -cJf "${ASSET_PATH}" -C "${PAYLOAD_STAGE}" am mcp-agent-mail streaming-sentinel.bin
+ASSET_BYTES="$(wc -c < "${ASSET_PATH}" | tr -d '[:space:]')"
+OLD_HTTP_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES=$((16 * 1024 * 1024))
+e2e_case_banner "mock release asset exceeds streaming threshold"
+e2e_save_artifact "mock_asset_size.txt" "asset_bytes=${ASSET_BYTES}
+old_http_body_limit_bytes=${OLD_HTTP_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES}
+streaming_sentinel_bytes=${STREAMING_SENTINEL_SIZE}"
+if [ "${ASSET_BYTES}" -gt "${OLD_HTTP_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES}" ]; then
+    e2e_pass "mock tarball exceeds old non-streaming body limit (${ASSET_BYTES} bytes)"
+else
+    e2e_fail "mock tarball should exceed old non-streaming body limit (${ASSET_BYTES} bytes)"
+    e2e_summary
+    exit 1
+fi
+
 ASSET_SHA="$(e2e_sha256 "${ASSET_PATH}")"
 printf "%s  %s\n" "${ASSET_SHA}" "${ASSET_NAME}" > "${ASSET_PATH}.sha256"
